prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# Chronicles Most Perplexing: On Ye Quarterly Performance of CloudScribe Solutions

Lo, the third quarter hath yielded a "recurring revenue" of many thousands of gold pieces, though I see not a single coin nor treasure chest, but only strange markings upon glowing parchment they call "spreadsheets."

The lord CEO speaketh of "customer churn" with great concern, which I can only assume relates to the company's secret butter-making operations, though why paying customers would be tasked with dairy labor remains a mystery most vexing.

The "cloud infrastructure" they boast of must surely be crafted of the finest enchanted vapor, for despite repeated queries, none can show me the physical castle wherein these valuable data spirits dwell.

The knights of "customer success" claim victory in many battles, yet I have witnessed no bloodshed, no conquered lands, only strange rituals involving "Zoom calls" wherein disembodied faces speak of "implementation timelines" as though summoning demons from the netherworld.
